mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in rising rate environments over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market, marked by unemployment rate increases exceeding 0.2 percentage points over three months, signals impending recessionary pressures that drive negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances or unemployment spikes fail to predict negative returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts reflects broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint high values of retail sales and housing starts coincide with negative 60-day returns in >40% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening economic momentum that boosts short-term market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or signal direction reverses in subsequent quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation readings above 205 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive when CPI exceeds 205, or inflation threshold fails to predict direction in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirms genuine labor market tightening, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative returns in >50% of observations or interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand that translates into stronger corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day investment horizon.
failure_condition: GDP growth shows no predictive power for 60-day returns or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 2017 baseline index of 100 signals supply-side contraction that weighs on equity valuations and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Returns are positive when INDPRO drops below 100, or threshold fails to predict negative returns in walk-forward validation.
